description_long: This cabin features a long, rectangular structure with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and aged appearance. The roof has a pronounced overhang on both long sides, providing some shelter to the walls below. There are no visible windows on any side, and the only apparent entrance is a small, recessed doorway on one of the short ends. The wood shows signs of exposure to the elements, with subtle variations in color and texture. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for rural or forested settings.
description: A rustic, elongated wooden cabin with a steep gable roof and weathered wood siding, featuring a single small entrance and no visible windows.
description_view_0: The long side of the cabin is dominated by the wide, shingled gable roof with a deep overhang and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_1: The short end reveals the triangular gable, horizontal wood siding, and a small, recessed entrance at the base.
description_view_2: The opposite long side mirrors the first, showing the full length of the roof and wall with no openings.
description_view_3: The other short end displays the gable peak and wood siding, but no entrance or windows.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch and wide overhangs, covered in weathered shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: No visible windows on any side; the cabin appears windowless from all views shown
attribute_door_placement: Single small door recessed at the base of one short end (gable side)
